sources.name: coprep
sources.title: Top Agentic AI Interview Questions for 2026: Beyond the Models
sources.topic: Agentic AI fundamentals — ReAct, GraphRAG, autonomous agents
key_points: ReAct is a cognitive architecture, not just a prompt template — it's reasoning+acting in loops
GraphRAG enables relationship traversal across entities via knowledge graphs (Cypher/GraphQL)
Autonomous agents need Planner, Executor, Memory (short+long term), and a Monitor/Critic module
Failure analysis must cover: tool failure, parsing ambiguity, hallucinated actions
Meta-cognition (self-correction, loop detection) is the hardest part of autonomous agents

sources.name: datacamp_rag
sources.title: Top 30 RAG Interview Questions and Answers for 2026
sources.topic: RAG interview questions (30 questions) — basic, intermediate, advanced, AI engineer
key_points: RAG = retriever + generator; retrieval methods: sparse (BM25/TF-IDF) vs dense (BERT/DPR)
Hybrid search combines BM25 speed with dense re-ranking accuracy
Chunking techniques: fixed-length, sentence, paragraph, semantic, sliding window, late chunking
CAG (Cache-Augmented Generation) vs RAG: CAG for static datasets, RAG for dynamic/fresh data
Advanced RAG types: Adaptive RAG, Agentic RAG, Corrective RAG (CRAG), Self-RAG
Production concerns: latency, data privacy (PII redaction, encryption, RBAC), monitoring

sources.name: novelvista
sources.title: Agentic AI Interview Questions & Answers (2026 Guide)
sources.topic: Agentic AI comprehensive — architecture, memory, multi-agent, safety
key_points: What makes an AI system 'agentic' vs just automated (autonomy + goal-orientation)
Goal-driven vs instruction-driven systems: goals allow adaptation, instructions are rigid
Agent memory: episodic (past experiences), semantic (world knowledge), procedural (how-to)
Multi-agent coordination: hierarchical, peer-to-peer, marketplace-based patterns
Agent safety: sandbox environments, human-in-the-loop, graduated permissions

sources.name: galileo_eval
sources.title: How to Build an Agent Evaluation Framework With Metrics, Rubrics, and Benchmarks
key_points: Trajectory metrics (how agent behaves) vs outcome metrics (what agent achieves)
Production needs both: outcome tells you if it works, trajectory tells you why it fails
Evaluation rubrics: accuracy, completeness, efficiency, safety, instruction following
Benchmarking against standard agent eval suites (GAIA, AgentBench, WebArena)
Continuous eval pipeline: regression tests on canonical scenarios before deployment

sources.name: thinking_co
sources.title: AI Agent Evaluation in Production (2026 Guide) | The Thinking Company
sources.topic: Production AI agent evaluation and testing
key_points: Why traditional testing fails for AI agents (non-deterministic outputs, emergent behaviors)
Key eval categories: functional correctness, robustness, latency, cost, safety
Human intervention rate metrics (how often humans must override or correct agent output)
Primary objective and accuracy thresholds must be defined upfront (95%? 99%?)
Monitoring: drift detection, cost tracking, latency SLOs, alerting on failure modes

sources.name: callsphere
sources.title: 7 Agentic AI & Multi-Agent System Interview Questions for 2026 | CallSphere Blog
key_points: Multi-agent architectures: supervisor-workers, swarm, debate/critic patterns
Agent memory systems: short-term (scratchpad), long-term (vector store/DB), shared memory
Critic-based evaluation: use a separate 'critic' agent to validate outputs against rubric
Agent communication protocols: structured messages vs natural language handoffs
Security concerns in multi-agent: prompt injection propagation, privilege escalation

sources.name: anthropic
sources.title: Building Effective AI Agents | Anthropic
sources.topic: Anthropic official guidance on building effective agents
key_points: Agents vs workflows: agents dynamically direct their own processes; workflows are predefined code paths
When to use frameworks: start simple, add complexity only when patterns repeat
Tools should use formats similar to what the model has seen naturally in text on the web
Test tool usage extensively: run many example inputs to surface mistakes before shipping
Key design patterns: prompt chaining, routing, parallelization, orchestrator-workers, evaluator-optimizer

sources.name: langchain_multi_agent
sources.title: Multi-agent - Docs by LangChain
key_points: Subagent pattern: main agent delegates to subagents as tool calls
Key design considerations: distributed dev (team autonomy), parallelization, multi-hop routing
Direct user interaction: some patterns allow subagents to converse directly with users
State management across agents: shared memory, message passing, checkpointing
LangGraph provides graph-based state machine for multi-agent orchestration
